patch.value: Performance baseline: list-open reads all 636 items front-matter on every invocation

pm list-open takes ~4.2s for 636 items due to full front-matter scan. Evaluate cached file manifest or incremental read strategy for large corpora. Benchmark: --version ~0.8s, health ~2.5s, search ~2.3s, list-open ~4.2s, semantic reindex ~34s for 636 items.

value.author: audit-agent
value.text: Audit 2026-05-02: listAllDocuments reads all files from all type directories, parses each with TOON/JSON parser, runs onRead hooks, then deduplicates by ID. For 636+ items this is O(n) reads. A manifest-based approach (tracking id->path->mtime) would enable incremental reads. Calendar listAllFrontMatter has the same issue. Sentry lazy-loading confirmed working (ensureSentryInit with _initDone guard). Embedding batch retry with exponential backoff confirmed correct.

value.author: audit-agent
value.text: 2026-05-02 audit: Performance measured - list-open ~0.95s, search ~1.58s (includes Ollama), context ~1.17s, calendar ~0.74s for 644 items. Acceptable but list-open could benefit from pagination/lazy-load for very large projects (1000+ items).

value.author: audit-agent
value.text: 2026-05-02 audit note: Performance issue confirmed but mitigated by current codebase size (647 items, sub-2s response). Will become important if tracker exceeds 1000+ items. No regression since last check.
